storage.value:
User Story

 

As a system user

I want to be able to create Customer Liability manually

So that I will have possibility to connect it to the customer and conclude payment process correctly

 

Design: Liability (adobe.com)

 

Acceptance criteria:

  1. Creating a customer liability is possible from Customer Liability's listing page by clicking on a 'Plus' button

  2. Clicking on a Plus button will open Customer Liability's creation page

  3. Check system user permission to be able to create a new customer liability

    1. Permission - Create Customer Liability

    2. Enable plus button when user has permission

    3. Disable plus button when user doesn't have permission

  4. Default state of Customer Liability object contains fields:

    1. Number

      1. Always disabled and empty on creation mode

      2. After creating a customer liability system should display its number with the following logic: Liability-ID of newly created liability (For example, Liability-56)

        1. In the given example, Liability is hardcoded prefix for number and 56 is ID of liability

    2. Accounting period

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. Accounting Periods should be displayed with its name

        2. It is possible to search accounting period with its name

        3. List should be sorted with its creation date

      3. By default, current month's accounting period should be displayed in this field

      4. It is possible to change the accounting period and select it from the past (For example, if current month is February and user selects January's Accounting Period which is currently open)

      5. System shouldn't display closed accounting periods in the dropdown

      6. System should display only open accounting periods in the dropdown

      7. liability object- Occurrence date can be outside of accounting period

      8. Creation date should be in current accounting period for all objects

    3. Creation date

      1. Mandatory

      2. Always disabled

      3. Automatically filled by the system

    4. occurrence date

      1. Liability Creating

      2. Mandator

      3. Always enable in creating mode

      4. Date picker

      5. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      6. Data should be displayed between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      7. It is possible to define date as current day or in the future or in the past

      8. should be restricted to be less or equal then due date

    5. Due date

      1. Mandatory

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. It is possible to define date as current day or in the future or in the past

    6. Date of full offset

      1. Always disabled

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. System should automatically set date when liability is covered fully by the offsetting process

    7. Applicable interest rate

      1. Optional

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List of interest rates are retrieved

        2. Values should be displayed with its names

        3. Only active values will be returned (deleted interest rates should be excluded)

        4. Order should be by creation date

        5. Should be possible to search with name

      3. If there is default interest rate defined in the system, such record should be set in the field by default

    8. Interest date from

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. It is possible to choose date from past/current day/future

      6. Interest rate applicable date from should be less or equal then Interest rate applicable date to

    9. Interest date to

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. It is possible to choose date from past/current day/future

      6. Interest rate applicable date to should be more or equal then Interest rate applicable date from

    10. Initial amount

      1. mandatory

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point. min value can be 0

      5. Automatically filled with ‘initial amount’ on creation

      6. Amount cannot be zero.

    11. Initial amount in other currency

      1. Disabled

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point

      5. When Currency and Initial amount are defined, system should calculate value from the Initial amount in other currency defined in the system with its exchange rate

    12. Current amount

      1. Disabled

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point

      5. generate from initial amount

    13. Current amount in other currency

      1. Disabled

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point

      5. When currency and Current amount are defined, system should calculate value from the Current amount in other currency defined in the system with its exchange rate

    14. Currency

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable from nomenclature

        2. Active values should be returned from nomenclature

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display default if returned from nomenclature

        5. Display search bar if number of items are more than 10 in the dropdown

      3. When currency is defined, is means that following field should be taken into consideration with this value:

        1. Initial amount

        2. Current amount

    15. Outgoing document from an external system

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    16. Basis for issuing

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    17. Number of income account

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    18. Cost center / Controlling order

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    19. Direct debit

      1. Checkbox

      2. Unselected by default

      3. Bank, BIC and Bank Account field should be optional when checkbox is not selected

      4. When Direct Debit checkbox is selected, fields connected to the bank should become mandatory:

        1. Bank

          1. Single select dropdown

          2. List of banks are manageable from the nomenclature

          3. Display search bar if number of items re more than 10 items in the dropdown list

          4. While typing search letter, list in dropdown should be filtered by search letter

        2. BIC

          1. Always disabled

          2. Text field

          3. Automatically filled after selecting BANK

        3. Bank Account

          1. Text field

          2. If first 2 symbols of IBAN are BG should cover current validation - BG + 2 symbols (0-9) +4 symbols (A-Z) + 6 symbols (0-9) + 8 symbols ( A-Z 0-9), Example: BG21TBCA12345601234567

          3. If first 2 symbols of IBAN are not BG use next validation - length from 14 to 34 symbols, first 2 symbols A-Z, rest symbols A-Z 0-9, Example: AZ12345678

    20. Additional liability information

      1. Optional

      2. Text box

      3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

      4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    21. Blocked for payment

      1. Optional

      2. Checkbox

      3. Unselected by default

      4. If user does not have permission for Blocked for Payment, checkbox should be disabled

      5. If checkbox is not selected, then following fields should be disabled:

        1. Blocked from date

        2. Blocked to date

        3. Reason

        4. Additional information

      6. When user selects the checkbox, fields should become enable:

        1. Blocked from date

          1. Mandatory

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is possible to define date as current day or in the future or in the past

          6. Blocked from date <= Blocked to date

        2. Blocked to date

          1. Optional

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible to choose date from the future

          7. Blocked to date >= Blocked from date

        3. Reason

          1. Mandatory

          2. Single select dropdown

            1. List is manageable from the Blocking Reasons nomenclature

            2. Display values from the nomenclature for which Blocked for payment option is selected

            3. Active values will be returned from the nomenclature

            4. Order should be considered

            5. Display default if returned from nomenclature 

        4. Additional information

          1. Optional

          2. Text box

          3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

          4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    22. Blocked for reminder letters

      1. Optional

      2. Checkbox

      3. Unselected by default

      4. If user does not have permission for Blocked for reminder letters, checkbox should be disabled

      5. If checkbox is not selected, then following fields should be disabled:

        1. Blocked from date

        2. Blocked to date

        3. Reason

        4. Additional information

      6. When user selects the checkbox, fields should become enable:

        1. Blocked from date

          1. Mandatory

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible date to be current day or in the future

          7. Blocked from date <= Blocked to date

        2. Blocked to date

          1. Optional

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible to choose date from the future

          7. Blocked to date >= Blocked from date

        3. Reason

          1. Mandatory

          2. Single select dropdown

            1. List is manageable from the Blocking Reasons nomenclature

            2. Display values from the nomenclature for which Blocked for reminder letters option is selected

            3. Active values will be returned from the nomenclature

            4. Order should be considered

            5. Display default if returned from nomenclature 

        4. Additional information

          1. Optional

          2. Text box

          3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

          4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    23. Blocked  for calculation of late payment fines/interests

      1. Optional

      2. Checkbox

      3. Unselected by default

      4. If user does not have permission for Blocked for calculation of late payment fines/interests, checkbox should be disabled

      5. If checkbox is not selected, then following fields should be disabled:

        1. Blocked from date

        2. Blocked to date

        3. Reason

        4. Additional information

      6. When user selects the checkbox, fields should become enable:

        1. Blocked from date

          1. Mandatory

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible date to be current day or in the future

          7. Blocked from date <= Blocked to date

        2. Blocked to date

          1. Optional

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible to choose date from the future

          7. Blocked to date >= Blocked from date

        3. Reason

          1. Mandatory

          2. Single select dropdown

            1. List is manageable from the Blocking Reasons nomenclature

            2. Display values from the nomenclature for which Blocked for calculation of late payment fines/interests option is selected

            3. Active values will be returned from the nomenclature

            4. Order should be considered

            5. Display default if returned from nomenclature 

        4. Additional information

          1. Optional

          2. Text box

          3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

          4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    24. Blocked for liabilities offsetting

      1. Optional

      2. Checkbox

      3. Unselected by default

      4. If user does not have permission for Blocked for liabilities offsetting, checkbox should be disabled

      5. If checkbox is not selected, then following fields should be disabled:

        1. Blocked from date

        2. Blocked to date

        3. Reason

        4. Additional information

      6. When user selects the checkbox, fields should become enable:

        1. Blocked from date

          1. Mandatory

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible date to be current day or in the future

          7. Blocked from date <= Blocked to date

        2. Blocked to date

          1. Optional

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible to choose date from the future

          7. Blocked to date >= Blocked from date

        3. Reason

          1. Mandatory

          2. Single select dropdown

            1. List is manageable from the Blocking Reasons nomenclature

            2. Display values from the nomenclature for which Blocked for liabilities offsetting option is selected

            3. Active values will be returned from the nomenclature

            4. Order should be considered

            5. Display default if returned from nomenclature 

        4. Additional information

          1. Optional

          2. Text box

          3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

          4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    25. Blocked for supply termination

      1. Optional

      2. Checkbox

      3. Unselected by default

      4. If user does not have permission for Blocked for supply termination, checkbox should be disabled

      5. If checkbox is not selected, then following fields should be disabled:

        1. Blocked from date

        2. Blocked to date

        3. Reason

        4. Additional information

      6. When user selects the checkbox, fields should become enable:

        1. Blocked from date

          1. Mandatory

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible date to be current day or in the future

          7. Blocked from date <= Blocked to date

        2. Blocked to date

          1. Optional

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible to choose date from the future

          7. Blocked to date >= Blocked from date

        3. Reason

          1. Mandatory

          2. Single select dropdown

            1. List is manageable from the Blocking Reason nomenclature

            2. Display values from the nomenclature for which Blocked for supply termination option is selected

            3. Active valued will be returned from the nomenclature

            4. Order should be considered

            5. Display default if returned from nomenclature

          3. Additional information

            1. Optional

            2. Text box

            3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

            4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    26. Sub-objects:

      1. Customer

        1. Mandatory

        2. It is possible to add only one customer to the liability

        3. Customer is added manually

        4. Adding customer is possible by clicking on a Plus button

          1. When clicking on the Plus button, modal window should be displayed with fields:

            1. Search bar - to search a customer and add it to the liability

              1. Search bar should have search button

            2. Text field - it is possible to type an UIC/Personal number and search for all customer in the system. It is possible to return a customer which is already connected to the liability

            3. Name - filled automatically based on the latest customer's version after clicking on Search button. If version is changed in version's dropdown name will be updated according to the version

              1. Customer is added with its ID to the Customer Liability, not with its Version

              2. It is possible to add customer with its any status to the liability

            4. Save - when user finds needed customer and clicks on this button, modal window should be closed, and the customer is added to the liability's creation page

            5. Cancel - modal window is closed and user stays on the liability's creation page

        5. It is possible to remove customer when clicking on remove icon

        6. It is possible to go to the customer's detail page when clicking on its name or an arrow

        7. After adding customer to the liability, it should be displayed with following format:

          1. For Private customer display in the list:

            1. Personal number

            2. Name

            3. Middle name (if exists)

            4. Surname

            5. e,g 1234567890 ( ivan ivan ivanov)

          2. For Legal entity display in the list:

            1. UIC

            2. Legal entity name + legal form (short)

            3. e.g 123456789 ( Energopro Georgia)

      2. Billing group

        1. Optional

        2. It is possible to add only one billing group to the liability

        3. Until customer is not added to the liability, billing group's sub-object should be disabled

        4. When user adds customer to the liability, only after that action it is possible to add billing group to the liability

        5. Billing group is added manually to the liability by clicking on the Plus button

          1. When clicking on Plus button modal window should be opened with one mandatory dropdown

            1. Billing group - in the dropdown menu values should be filtered according to the customer. Values should be displayed according to all contracts for this customer.

              1. Values should be displayed with its number

              2. For example: 0001

        6. It is possible to remove billing group when clicking on remove icon

        7. It is possible to go to the contract's detailed page when clicking on billing group's name or an arrow

        8. After adding billing group to the liability, it should be displayed with following format:

          1. Number

          2. For example, 0001

      3. Alternative recipient of an invoice

        1. Optional

        2. It is possible to add only one alternative recipient of an invoice to the liability

        3. If billing group is added to the liability and alternative recipient of an invoice is defined there, such record should be displayed automatically,It should not be possible to delete or change a customer that is automatically filled.

        4. If alternative recipient of an invoice is empty in the billing group, it should be possible to add it in the sub object manually

        5. If billing group is not added to the liability, in this case alternative recipient of an invoice can be added manually to the liability by clicking on a Plus button

          1. When clicking on a Plus button, modal window should be opened with fields:

            1. Search bar - to search a customer and add it to the liability

              1. Search bar should have search button

            2. Text field - it is possible to type an UIC/Personal number and search for all customer in the system. It is possible to return a customer which is already connected to the liability

            3. Name - filled automatically based on the latest customer's version after clicking on Search button. If version is changed in version's dropdown name will be updated according to the version

              1. Alternative Recipient for an Invoice should be added with its ID not with its Version

              2. It is possible to add customer with its any status to the liability

            4. Save - when user finds needed customer and clicks on this button, modal window should be closed, and the customer is added to the liability's creation page

            5. Cancel - modal window is closed and user stays on the liability's creation page

        6. It is possible to remove alternative recipient of an invoice when clicking on remove icon

        7. It is possible to go to the customer's detail page when clicking on its name or an arrow

        8. After adding alternative recipient of an invoice to the liability, it should be displayed with following format:

          1. Customer's UIC/personal number and its name summary in the brackets

      4. Outgoing document

        1. Shouldn’t display when Customer Liability is created manually or via Import

        2. Sub-object will be displayed in the case when the Customer Liability is created automatically based on the Invoice, Debit Note, Deposit, action, Late Payment Fine, Rescheduling

        3. format (Name, prefix, Number)
          e.g.
          fine-PV-0022456

      5. Offsetting

        1. Always disabled

        2. System should automatically display related objects according to the offsetting process

        3. List of the objects which can be attached to the liability:

          1. Payment

          2. Receivable

          3. Deposit

        4. Payments, receivables and deposits should be added to the liability with the following logic:

          1. ID-Date-Amount and Currency

  5. Clicking on 'Save' button will check all the validations (frontend and backend)

    1. If all the validations are successful, then system should create new liability and display it in the listing page

    2. If frontend validations are not successful, system should display an error message with proper field

    3. If an error happens in backend, system should display an error message retrieved from the backend

  6. When clicking on 'Cancel' or 'X' button warning pop-up message should appear: 'Are you sure you want to cancel?' Yes/No

    1. Yes - will close pop-up and user is returned to the listing page

    2. No - will close pop-up and user stays on the current page

    3. Warning message should be managed from the system settings

  7. Audit Log should be created

  8. Liability should not be created with amount zero

Customer Feedbacks: https://oppa-support.atlassian.net/browse/PDT-2508

https://oppa-support.atlassian.net/browse/PDT-2474

User Story

 

As a system user

I want to be able to create Customer Liability manually

So that I will have possibility to connect it to the customer and conclude payment process correctly

 

Design: Liability (adobe.com)

 

Acceptance criteria:

  1. Creating a customer liability is possible from Customer Liability's listing page by clicking on a 'Plus' button

  2. Clicking on a Plus button will open Customer Liability's creation page

  3. Check system user permission to be able to create a new customer liability

    1. Permission - Create Customer Liability

    2. Enable plus button when user has permission

    3. Disable plus button when user doesn't have permission

  4. Default state of Customer Liability object contains fields:

    1. Number

      1. Always disabled and empty on creation mode

      2. After creating a customer liability system should display its number with the following logic: Liability-ID of newly created liability (For example, Liability-56)

        1. In the given example, Liability is hardcoded prefix for number and 56 is ID of liability

    2. Accounting period

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. Accounting Periods should be displayed with its name

        2. It is possible to search accounting period with its name

        3. List should be sorted with its creation date

      3. By default, current month's accounting period should be displayed in this field

      4. It is possible to change the accounting period and select it from the past (For example, if current month is February and user selects January's Accounting Period which is currently open)

      5. System shouldn't display closed accounting periods in the dropdown

      6. System should display only open accounting periods in the dropdown

      7. liability object- Occurrence date can be outside of accounting period

      8. Creation date should be in current accounting period for all objects

    3. Creation date

      1. Mandatory

      2. Always disabled

      3. Automatically filled by the system

    4. occurrence date

      1. Liability Creating

      2. Mandator

      3. Always enable in creating mode

      4. Date picker

      5. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      6. Data should be displayed between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      7. It is possible to define date as current day or in the future or in the past

      8. should be restricted to be less or equal then due date

    5. Due date

      1. Mandatory

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. It is possible to define date as current day or in the future or in the past

    6. Date of full offset

      1. Always disabled

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. System should automatically set date when liability is covered fully by the offsetting process

    7. Applicable interest rate

      1. Optional

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List of interest rates are retrieved

        2. Values should be displayed with its names

        3. Only active values will be returned (deleted interest rates should be excluded)

        4. Order should be by creation date

        5. Should be possible to search with name

      3. If there is default interest rate defined in the system, such record should be set in the field by default

    8. Interest date from

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. It is possible to choose date from past/current day/future

      6. Interest rate applicable date from should be less or equal then Interest rate applicable date to

    9. Interest date to

      1. Optional

      2. Date picker

      3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

      4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

      5. It is possible to choose date from past/current day/future

      6. Interest rate applicable date to should be more or equal then Interest rate applicable date from

    10. Initial amount

      1. mandatory

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point. min value can be 0

      5. Automatically filled with ‘initial amount’ on creation

      6. Amount cannot be zero.

    11. Initial amount in other currency

      1. Disabled

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point

      5. When Currency and Initial amount are defined, system should calculate value from the Initial amount in other currency defined in the system with its exchange rate

    12. Current amount

      1. Disabled

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point

      5. generate from initial amount

    13. Current amount in other currency

      1. Disabled

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: 0-9 . ,

      4. Length: 1-32 characters, 2 digits after decimal point

      5. When currency and Current amount are defined, system should calculate value from the Current amount in other currency defined in the system with its exchange rate

    14. Currency

      1. Mandatory

      2. Single select dropdown

        1. List is manageable from nomenclature

        2. Active values should be returned from nomenclature

        3. Order should be considered

        4. Display default if returned from nomenclature

        5. Display search bar if number of items are more than 10 in the dropdown

      3. When currency is defined, is means that following field should be taken into consideration with this value:

        1. Initial amount

        2. Current amount

    15. Outgoing document from an external system

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    16. Basis for issuing

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    17. Number of income account

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    18. Cost center / Controlling order

      1. Optional

      2. Text field

      3. Allowed symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9

      4. Length: 1-512 characters

    19. Direct debit

      1. Checkbox

      2. Unselected by default

      3. Bank, BIC and Bank Account field should be optional when checkbox is not selected

      4. When Direct Debit checkbox is selected, fields connected to the bank should become mandatory:

        1. Bank

          1. Single select dropdown

          2. List of banks are manageable from the nomenclature

          3. Display search bar if number of items re more than 10 items in the dropdown list

          4. While typing search letter, list in dropdown should be filtered by search letter

        2. BIC

          1. Always disabled

          2. Text field

          3. Automatically filled after selecting BANK

        3. Bank Account

          1. Text field

          2. If first 2 symbols of IBAN are BG should cover current validation - BG + 2 symbols (0-9) +4 symbols (A-Z) + 6 symbols (0-9) + 8 symbols ( A-Z 0-9), Example: BG21TBCA12345601234567

          3. If first 2 symbols of IBAN are not BG use next validation - length from 14 to 34 symbols, first 2 symbols A-Z, rest symbols A-Z 0-9, Example: AZ12345678

    20. Additional liability information

      1. Optional

      2. Text box

      3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

      4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    21. Blocked for payment

      1. Optional

      2. Checkbox

      3. Unselected by default

      4. If user does not have permission for Blocked for Payment, checkbox should be disabled

      5. If checkbox is not selected, then following fields should be disabled:

        1. Blocked from date

        2. Blocked to date

        3. Reason

        4. Additional information

      6. When user selects the checkbox, fields should become enable:

        1. Blocked from date

          1. Mandatory

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is possible to define date as current day or in the future or in the past

          6. Blocked from date <= Blocked to date

        2. Blocked to date

          1. Optional

          2. Date picker

          3. Format: DD.MM.YYYY

          4. Data should be displayed in between 01.01.1990 to 31.12.2090

          5. It is not possible to choose date from the past

          6. It is possible to choose date from the future

          7. Blocked to date >= Blocked from date

        3. Reason

          1. Mandatory

          2. Single select dropdown

            1. List is manageable from the Blocking Reasons nomenclature

            2. Display values from the nomenclature for which Blocked for payment option is selected

            3. Active values will be returned from the nomenclature

            4. Order should be considered

            5. Display default if returned from nomenclature 

        4. Additional information

          1. Optional

          2. Text box

          3. All symbols are allowed. Should not be only spaces. Spaces at the beginning and at the end should be trimmed

          4. Length: 1-2048 characters

    26. Sub-objects:

      1. Customer

        1. Mandatory

        2. It is possible to add only one customer to the liability

        3. Customer is added manually

        4. Adding customer is possible by clicking on a Plus button

          1. When clicking on the Plus button, modal window should be displayed with fields:

            1. Search bar - to search a customer and add it to the liability

              1. Search bar should have search button

            2. Text field - it is possible to type an UIC/Personal number and search for all customer in the system. It is possible to return a customer which is already connected to the liability

            3. Name - filled automatically based on the latest customer's version after clicking on Search button. If version is changed in version's dropdown name will be updated according to the version

              1. Customer is added with its ID to the Customer Liability, not with its Version

              2. It is possible to add customer with its any status to the liability

            4. Save - when user finds needed customer and clicks on this button, modal window should be closed, and the customer is added to the liability's creation page

            5. Cancel - modal window is closed and user stays on the liability's creation page

        5. It is possible to remove customer when clicking on remove icon

        6. It is possible to go to the customer's detail page when clicking on its name or an arrow

        7. After adding customer to the liability, it should be displayed with following format:

          1. For Private customer display in the list:

            1. Personal number

            2. Name

            3. Middle name (if exists)

            4. Surname

            5. e,g 1234567890 ( ivan ivan ivanov)

          2. For Legal entity display in the list:

            1. UIC

            2. Legal entity name + legal form (short)

            3. e.g 123456789 ( Energopro Georgia)

      2. Billing group

        1. Optional

        2. It is possible to add only one billing group to the liability

        3. Until customer is not added to the liability, billing group's sub-object should be disabled

        4. When user adds customer to the liability, only after that action it is possible to add billing group to the liability

        5. Billing group is added manually to the liability by clicking on the Plus button

          1. When clicking on Plus button modal window should be opened with one mandatory dropdown

            1. Billing group - in the dropdown menu values should be filtered according to the customer. Values should be displayed according to all contracts for this customer.

              1. Values should be displayed with its number

              2. For example: 0001

        6. It is possible to remove billing group when clicking on remove icon

        7. It is possible to go to the contract's detailed page when clicking on billing group's name or an arrow

        8. After adding billing group to the liability, it should be displayed with following format:

          1. Number

          2. For example, 0001

      3. Alternative recipient of an invoice

        1. Optional

        2. It is possible to add only one alternative recipient of an invoice to the liability

        3. If billing group is added to the liability and alternative recipient of an invoice is defined there, such record should be displayed automatically,It should not be possible to delete or change a customer that is automatically filled.

        4. If alternative recipient of an invoice is empty in the billing group, it should be possible to add it in the sub object manually

        5. If billing group is not added to the liability, in this case alternative recipient of an invoice can be added manually to the liability by clicking on a Plus button

          1. When clicking on a Plus button, modal window should be opened with fields:

            1. Search bar - to search a customer and add it to the liability

              1. Search bar should have search button

            2. Text field - it is possible to type an UIC/Personal number and search for all customer in the system. It is possible to return a customer which is already connected to the liability

            3. Name - filled automatically based on the latest customer's version after clicking on Search button. If version is changed in version's dropdown name will be updated according to the version

              1. Alternative Recipient for an Invoice should be added with its ID not with its Version

              2. It is possible to add customer with its any status to the liability

            4. Save - when user finds needed customer and clicks on this button, modal window should be closed, and the customer is added to the liability's creation page

            5. Cancel - modal window is closed and user stays on the liability's creation page

        6. It is possible to remove alternative recipient of an invoice when clicking on remove icon

        7. It is possible to go to the customer's detail page when clicking on its name or an arrow

        8. After adding alternative recipient of an invoice to the liability, it should be displayed with following format:

          1. Customer's UIC/personal number and its name summary in the brackets

      4. Outgoing document

        1. Shouldn’t display when Customer Liability is created manually or via Import

        2. Sub-object will be displayed in the case when the Customer Liability is created automatically based on the Invoice, Debit Note, Deposit, action, Late Payment Fine, Rescheduling

        3. format (Name, prefix, Number)
          e.g.
          fine-PV-0022456

      5. Offsetting

        1. Always disabled

        2. System should automatically display related objects according to the offsetting process

        3. List of the objects which can be attached to the liability:

          1. Payment

          2. Receivable

          3. Deposit

        4. Payments, receivables and deposits should be added to the liability with the following logic:

          1. ID-Date-Amount and Currency

  5. Clicking on 'Save' button will check all the validations (frontend and backend)

    1. If all the validations are successful, then system should create new liability and display it in the listing page

    2. If frontend validations are not successful, system should display an error message with proper field

    3. If an error happens in backend, system should display an error message retrieved from the backend

  6. When clicking on 'Cancel' or 'X' button warning pop-up message should appear: 'Are you sure you want to cancel?' Yes/No

    1. Yes - will close pop-up and user is returned to the listing page

    2. No - will close pop-up and user stays on the current page

    3. Warning message should be managed from the system settings

  7. Audit Log should be created

  8. Liability should not be created with amount zero
